mechanism_narrative: A decline in FEDFUNDS transmits through lower discount rates and easier financial conditions, lifting equity valuations and producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if FEDFUNDS decreases are followed by negative 20-day SPX returns in more than 55% of rolling 3-year windows or if the sign of the conditional return is not positive after rate cuts.

mechanism_narrative: A rising UNRATE above a short-run threshold signals weakening household income and demand, which compresses earnings expectations and leads to negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises by at least 0.3 pp over 3 months
failure_condition: Reject if threshold breaches in UNRATE are followed by positive 20-day SPX returns in more than 60% of cases or if the average forward return is not below the unconditional mean.

mechanism_narrative: When RSAFS and PAYEMS both accelerate together, higher retail demand and payroll growth confirm broad consumption strength, which supports positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if joint RSAFS-PAYEMS acceleration does not outperform either series alone, or if positive 5-day SPX returns occur in less than 52% of joint-signal instances.

mechanism_narrative: A FEDFUNDS level above 4.5% indicates restrictive policy that raises discount rates and tightens credit, which should produce negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if returns after high-FEDFUNDS regimes are not negative over the next 60 days or if the hit rate for negative returns falls below 55% across rolling 2-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: A rise in INDPRO with stable or falling CPIAUCSL indicates improving real supply capacity without new inflation pressure, which supports positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if INDPRO uptrends paired with flat or declining CPIAUCSL fail to produce positive 20-day SPX returns, or if the signal becomes negative in any sustained 2-year window.

mechanism_narrative: A downturn in HOUST signals weakening housing demand and construction activity, which propagates to earnings expectations and produces negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: HOUST falls more than 5% from its 3-month average
failure_condition: Reject if HOUST drawdowns are not followed by below-average 60-day SPX returns or if the signal’s directional accuracy remains below 50% for two consecutive years.

mechanism_narrative: In a payroll deceleration regime, a simultaneous rise in UNRATE and slowdown in PAYEMS identifies labor-market deterioration that should lead to negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if joint worsening in UNRATE and PAYEMS does not predict negative 20-day SPX returns, or if the interaction term has no incremental explanatory power over either series alone.

mechanism_narrative: A synchronized expansion in GDPC1 and RSAFS confirms broad final demand growth, which should support positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days as earnings revisions turn upward.
failure_condition: Reject if GDP and retail sales expansions do not lead to positive 60-day SPX returns, or if the conditional hit rate is not materially above the unconditional benchmark over any 3-year rolling sample.
